About Me

Building interactive worlds,
one Blueprint at a time.

I'm a game development student with a deep focus on Unreal Engine 5. I was the sole developer on Ember – The Near Nowhere, a narrative puzzle game where I designed and built every gameplay system — inventory management, puzzle mechanics, cutscene triggers, object interaction, and more — all in UE5 Blueprints within a 2.5 month sprint.

My workflow is cross-disciplinary by necessity. Working alongside designers, 3D modellers, and story writers taught me how to translate creative visions into working, playable systems. I take ownership of technical problems and see them through to completion — even under tight deadlines and scope pressure.

I also conducted live playtesting at a public mall showcase, observing real players and documenting 6 distinct UX issues with engineering solutions. This kind of thinking — shipping something real, watching people play it, and knowing how to fix it — is what drives me as a developer.

I'm actively seeking a game development internship where I can contribute on day one, deepen my UE5 and C++ skills, and grow into a professional developer.

● Completed · Public Showcase · 2024

Ember –
The Near Nowhere

Narrative Puzzle Adventure · Unreal Engine 5

Diya — a girl caught between the living and dead realms — must fulfill the unfinished wishes of wandering souls to restore the balance of life and death. Set in 1900s Italy, this emotionally-driven puzzle adventure has no combat, no dialogue — only environmental storytelling, memory fragments, and quiet human moments.

I was the sole developer on a 6-person team. Every gameplay system — inventory, puzzles, object interaction, cutscenes, realm transitions — was designed, built, and debugged by me in UE5 Blueprints over 2.5 months.

Engine
Unreal Engine 5.6

Gameplay Systems I Built
Inventory System
Full item tracking, slot management, and persistent state across level transitions. Core to all puzzle interactions.
Pick Up & Place
Physics-aware object interaction — players collect, carry, and place items with spatial precision.
Puzzle Logic
Multi-stage puzzles of 3 types: rainbow color lock, bead shape sort, and 3-valve water pressure system.
Cutscene Triggers
Event-driven cinematic sequences using UE5 Level Sequencer, fired by Blueprint gameplay events.
Object Highlighting
Proximity-based interactable highlight system drawing player attention to key objects in the environment.
Crafting System
Item combination system. Partially complete — scoped too late in production. Documented for iteration.
Realm Switching
Seamless Living World ↔ Upper Realm transitions with full environmental and lighting changes.
Memory Fragments
Collectible narrative items triggering visual story moments and emotional flashbacks through the world.
Interaction System
Context-sensitive E-key input — short press to collect items, long press to execute actions in the world.

Live Playtesting — Mall Showcase

Observed real players at a public event. Documented every UX issue and proposed concrete engineering solutions.

Issue 01
Players skipped to Level 2 before completing Level 1
→ Proposed Fix
Blueprint progression gate — Level 2 portal set to non-interactable until a completion Boolean flag from Level 1 is set to true
Issue 02
Players couldn't locate the door handle — a critical interactable
→ Proposed Fix
Pulsing emissive highlight that intensifies on proximity, paired with a subtle audio cue. Optional Niagara particle shimmer.
Issue 03
Players found key and stick before handle, soft-locking puzzle sequence
→ Proposed Fix
Blueprint state machine — objects are non-interactable until prerequisite flags are satisfied in the correct order.
Issue 04
Players wandered outside the intended play area when stuck
→ Proposed Fix
Invisible boundary volumes with soft atmospheric fog at edges — organic containment that fits the liminal world aesthetic.
Issue 05
Players asked for a tutorial — core controls weren't intuitive
→ Proposed Fix
Contextual 30-second in-world onboarding at the Upper Realm opening — teaches interaction through guided prompts, not a UI menu.
Issue 06
Objective markers drifted off-screen and became invisible
→ Proposed Fix
UMG screen-edge clamping — off-screen markers become anchored edge indicators showing distance. Standard AAA HUD pattern.
